title: Generic containers in roadmap/queries + project-aware render
description:
De-hardcode type==epic in internal/query/roadmap.go and plan.go: treat containment generically, distinguish orchestrated containers (epics) from passive ones (buckets/projects), derive project-ness from 'contains a container' (use T1 helpers). Make roadmap render walk the hierarchy and consume the T2 rollup. Backwards-compat is critical: a project-less epic roadmap must render identically. Design §1,§5,§9.

Files: internal/query/roadmap.go, internal/query/plan.go, cmd/tk/cmd/roadmap.go.
notes:
2026-06-20 14:05 - Consolidation: a new BuildChildIndex (map[string][]string) in internal/query/hierarchy.go duplicates the existing private buildChildIndex (map[string]bool) in roadmap.go/plan.go. When you rewrite roadmap/plan for generic containers, consolidate onto the public one.
2026-06-20 14:20 - runner-state: runner=claude branch=worktree-agent-a8bdd42c186389179
acceptance_criteria: tk roadmap renders nested projects and excludes passive buckets from orchestration nodes; childless epics still surface as needs-planning; ALL existing roadmap/plan tests stay green (backwards compat)
closed_reason: Completed: de-hardcoded type==epic in roadmap/plan; buckets excluded from nodes, projects grouped via Role+Parent with descendant rollup; backwards-compat verified (no existing test expectations changed)
